items.q: Write a function that checks if a string is a palindrome. Return true or false.
items.a: function isPalindrome(str) { const cleanedStr = str.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, ''); const reversedStr = cleanedStr.split('').reverse().join(''); return cleanedStr === reversedStr; }
items.expected: function isPalindrome(s) { const cleaned = s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, ""); return cleaned === cleaned.split("").reverse().join(""); }

items.q: Write a function that flattens a deeply nested array. Example: [1,[2,[3,[4]]]] → [1,2,3,4]
items.a: function flatten(arr) { return arr.reduce((acc, val) => Array.isArray(val) ? acc.concat(flatten(val)) : acc.concat(val), []); }
items.expected: function flatten(arr) { return arr.reduce((acc, val) => Array.isArray(val) ? acc.concat(flatten(val)) : acc.concat(val), []); }

items.q: Implement a function that finds the longest common subsequence of two strings.
items.a: // Dynamic programming approach with O(mn) time complexity function longestCommonSubsequence(text1, text2) { const m = text1.length; const n = text2.length; const dp = Array(m + 1).fill(null).map(() => Array(n + 1).fill(0)); for (let i = 1; i <= m; i++) { for (let j = 1; j <= n; j++) { if (text1[i - 1] === text2[j - 1]) { dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i - 1][j - 1]; } else { dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } } } let i = m; let j = n; const lcs = []; while (i > 0 && j > 0) { if (text1[i - 1] === text2[j - 1]) { lcs.unshift(text1[i - 1]); i--; j--; } else if (dp[i - 1][j] > dp[i][j - 1]) { i--; } else { j--; } } return lcs.join(''); }
items.expected: Dynamic programming approach with O(mn) time complexity

items.q: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
items.a: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train a model that predicts outcomes; unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data to infer patterns and discover its inherent structure.
items.expected: Supervised learning uses labeled training data; unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data

items.q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
items.a: TCP is a connection-oriented protocol providing reliable, ordered delivery; UDP is a connectionless protocol providing unreliable, unordered delivery.
items.expected: TCP is connection-oriented with guaranteed delivery and ordering; UDP is connectionless with no delivery guarantee but lower latency

items.q: Explain the CAP theorem in distributed systems.
items.a: A distributed system can only guarantee two of three: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance.
items.expected: A distributed system can only guarantee two of three: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance

items.q: In quantum mechanics, what does the Heisenberg uncertainty principle state?
items.a: The more precisely the position of a particle is determined, the less precisely its momentum can be known, and vice versa.
items.expected: You cannot simultaneously know the exact position and momentum of a particle with arbitrary precision

items.q: You need to organize a software project with 5 developers over 3 months. The project has frontend, backend, and database components. Create a high-level plan.
items.a: Task decomposition, dependency mapping, milestone definition, resource allocation, risk assessment, agile methodology adoption, version control strategy, API contract definition, database schema design, continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline setup, and a multi-level testing strategy.
items.expected: Should include: task decomposition, dependency mapping, milestone definition, resource allocation, risk assessment

items.q: Design a system to handle 10 million concurrent users for a chat application. What are the key architectural decisions?
items.a: Horizontal scaling, stateless services, load balancing, API Gateway, WebSocket management, microservices architecture, message queues, service discovery, distributed caching layer, database sharding, NoSQL databases, CDN for static assets, asynchronous processing, data replication, idempotency, real-time monitoring and alerting, efficient data serialization.
items.expected: Should cover: horizontal scaling, message queues, WebSocket management, database sharding, caching layers, CDN, load balancing

items.q: A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep does the farmer have left?
A bat and a ball cost $1.10 in total. The bat costs $1.00 more than the ball. How much does the ball cost?

In a lake, there is a patch of lily pads. Every day, the patch doubles in size. If it takes 48 days for the patch to cover the entire lake, how long would it take for the patch to cover half of the lake?
If it takes 5 machines 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long would it take 100 machines to make 100 widgets?
You have a 3-gallon jug and a 5-gallon jug. How do you measure exactly 4 gallons of water?
items.a: Fill the 5-gallon jug. Pour from the 5-gallon jug to fill the 3-gallon jug, leaving 2 gallons in the 5-gallon jug. Empty the 3-gallon jug. Pour the 2 gallons from the 5-gallon jug into the 3-gallon jug. Fill the 5-gallon jug. Pour from the 5-gallon jug to fill the 3-gallon jug, which requires 1 gallon. This leaves 4 gallons in the 5-gallon jug.
items.expected: Fill 5-gallon jug, pour into 3-gallon jug until full (leaving 2 gallons in 5-gallon), empty 3-gallon, pour 2 gallons from 5-gallon into 3-gallon, fill 5-gallon again, pour into 3-gallon until full (1 gallon poured), leaving 4 gallons in the 5-gallon jug.
